title: cooling_system_failure
description: A datacenter cooling unit fails, causing rising temperatures that threaten server hardware.

timeline.title: Temperature Alert Triggered
timeline.description: Environmental monitoring detects rack temperatures rising above 27 degrees C threshold. CRAC unit alarm is active.

timeline.title: CRAC Unit Failure Confirmed
timeline.description: Facilities team confirms a computer room air conditioning unit has failed. Temperatures are rising 2 degrees C per minute.

timeline.title: Critical Threshold Approaching
timeline.description: Rack temperatures reach 35 degrees C. Servers begin thermal throttling. Some hardware starts shutting down for self-protection.

timeline.title: Emergency Cooling Requested
timeline.description: Emergency portable cooling units are requested while HVAC engineers are dispatched.

timeline.title: Non-Critical Workloads Migrated
timeline.description: Non-critical servers are gracefully shut down or migrated to reduce heat generation.

timeline.title: Cooling Restored
timeline.description: Portable units lower temperatures. Primary CRAC unit is repaired. All servers operational.

node-1.title: Datacenter Temperature Rising
node-1.situation: Environmental monitoring shows rack temperatures rising rapidly after a CRAC unit failure. Current temperature is 33 degrees C and climbing. Server self-protection shutdowns have begun. You have authority to initiate emergency procedures.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the datacenter thermal emergency procedure: notify facilities for HVAC repair, request portable cooling units, and prepare to gracefully shut down non-critical systems.

options.label: B
options.text: Open all datacenter doors to allow ambient building air to cool the racks.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for the CRAC unit to self-recover before taking any action.

node-2.title: Emergency Cooling Prioritization
node-2.situation: Facilities confirms repair will take 90 minutes. Portable coolers will arrive in 20 minutes. Temperature is now 38 degrees C. You must decide which workloads to shut down to reduce heat load while protecting critical services.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Using the CMDB, identify and gracefully shut down non-critical servers (dev/test/batch), while keeping production and DR systems running, to reduce thermal load until cooling is restored.

options.label: B
options.text: Shut down all servers immediately including production systems to protect hardware from thermal damage.

node-3.title: Portable Cooling Deployment
node-3.situation: Non-critical servers are shut down. Portable coolers have arrived. Temperature has stabilized at 41 degrees C but is no longer climbing. You need to position the portable units for maximum effectiveness.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Position portable coolers to direct cold air into the hottest aisles using hot-aisle/cold-aisle containment principles, and continuously monitor temperatures until the primary unit is repaired.

options.label: B
options.text: Place all portable coolers in one corner of the room and assume they will cool the entire space evenly.

node-4.title: Recovery and Restart
node-4.situation: The primary CRAC unit is repaired. Temperatures have dropped back to 24 degrees C. You need to safely restore the shut-down systems and validate the environment.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Gradually power on non-critical servers in staged batches to avoid a power surge, verify hardware health after the thermal event, and document the incident with a review of cooling redundancy.

options.label: B
options.text: Power on all servers simultaneously as quickly as possible to restore full capacity.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You responded to the cooling failure by activating emergency procedures, selectively shutting down non-critical workloads, deploying portable cooling effectively, and overseeing a staged recovery once primary cooling was restored.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
